portfolio_health_check.first_reaction: Useful if it really shows my whole situation and not just another fancy chart. I would look, but carefully.
portfolio_health_check.strongest_appeal: Seeing whole-portfolio risk, concentration, currency exposure, and whether my income-focused holdings are giving me more risk than I realize.
portfolio_health_check.biggest_concern: That the bank uses the analysis as a more sophisticated way to steer me toward new products or to push me into linking all outside assets too early.
portfolio_health_check.what_they_understand: She understands that the feature is meant to show hidden risk across all holdings, test what happens under stress scenarios, and flag when her portfolio may not match her stated conservative profile.
portfolio_health_check.what_they_misunderstand: She may initially assume the tool can tell her exactly what to buy next, or that a bad stress-test result means immediate danger rather than a scenario-based warning. She may also not naturally separate market risk, rate risk, and credit risk unless the feature explains them plainly.
data_they_would_share: Basic information about investment objective and income needs
Possibly manual summary details of outside holdings
Maybe MPF category-level information if the purpose is explained clearly
data_they_would_not_share: Immediate full login-based access to every outside financial account
Open-ended permission for broad marketing use of aggregated data
portfolio_health_check.preferred_explanation_format: A short headline conclusion, followed by plain-language bullets in Cantonese-friendly wording, then simple charts showing concentration and stress impact, with a downloadable report she can review before speaking to RM.
portfolio_health_check.whether_rm_needed: Yes, but not at the first moment. She wants to see the analysis herself first, then use RM interpretation to confirm whether any action is genuinely needed.
portfolio_health_check.whether_they_would_pay: Maybe, but only for a deeper version. She expects a basic assessment to be free inside the bank app and would consider paying a modest one-time fee if the output is specific, printable, and comes with a proper review discussion.
portfolio_health_check.what_would_make_them_try: A free first analysis using existing bank assets, clear explanation that this is not auto-trading, proof that it covers actual portfolio weak points such as sector or currency concentration, and reassurance that using it will not automatically trigger sales pressure.
portfolio_health_check.what_would_make_them_abandon: Jargon-heavy results, generic risk scores, pressure to link all outside holdings before seeing value, immediate product recommendations, or a feeling that the stress tests are being used to scare her into transacting.
